从塞尔维亚导弹指挥官佐尔坦·达尼（Zoltan Dani）击落美国战斗机飞行员戴尔·泽尔科（Dale Zelko）的飞机那一刻起，两人就陷入了复杂的生死关系。
  离开贝尔格莱德后，您会被及时送回。塞尔维亚的首都是一个现代化的大都市，但是在农村，驴子仍然沿着鹅卵石堆砌，过去的农民将农产品推向市场。
 2011年，我在这里拜访了一家面包店Zoltan Dani。这家面包店位于Skorenovac镇，坐落在Zoltan长大的房子旁边的一座古老建筑中。当我走进去时，我发现他穿着面粉，戴着面包师的帽子和围裙，在一张大桌子上伸展面团。他笑了笑，向我招呼。我向他致敬，阻止了他的前进。他向我致敬，然后我们拥抱了。感觉再自然不过了。他就像我的兄弟一样，这个男人十二年前曾试图杀死我。
 那时，我是塞尔维亚冲突第一周的F-117隐形战斗机飞行员。那是1999年。我的任务是：炸毁防御最深，防御力最强的目标，深入敌国领土。这是一项了不起的工作。我知道战争另一端的人民也有同样的感觉。但是我无法考虑它们。我试图将目标视为混凝土和钢铁，没有个人依恋，没有人为因素。那是我的生存机制。
 而且有效。战争的第一天晚上，我有两个目标，两个都被击中。我在第三天晚上飞行，这也很成功。我在第四天晚上的目标是战略目标清单上的第一名。整条路线都由热导导弹，雷达制导导弹，高射炮等各种令人讨厌的东西保卫。
 隐形技术不是看不见的技术。这只会使飞机更难被发现。因此，在进入塞尔维亚领空之前的第四个晚上，我进行了一次隐形检查。我关掉灯，打开天线，然后关掉收音机和应答器，任何可能会丢掉我位置的发射器或发射器。在第四天晚上，我来到边境，只是等到最后一刻才关掉收音机，急切地想接那个电话：我们平安地弄清楚了。您可以返回基地。我没有接到那通电话。
  我飞到塞尔维亚，击中目标，然后开始返回意大利基地。在两枚SA3导弹穿过云层之前，我才看到它们。 
导弹以三倍于声速的速度移动，因此没有太多时间做出反应。就在第一枚导弹到达我之前，我闭上了眼睛，转过头，期待着那次撞击。我知道会有火球，而且我不想被蒙蔽。我感到第一个飞过我的身旁，以至于它震撼了飞机。然后我睁开眼睛，转过头，还有另一枚导弹。影响是暴力的。一阵巨大的光与热吞没了我的飞机，炸毁了左机翼，将飞机卷成一团。
 如果您乘坐的飞机遇到了一些湍流，并且脚部感到有些轻微的光线，那么您的瞬间重量为零。我的体重是负七克。我的身体被从座椅上朝天篷拉出。当我竭尽全力到达弹射手柄时，一个念头浮现在我的脑海：这真的，真的，真的很糟糕。
  从我拉出弹出手柄的那一刻起，到完全膨胀的降落伞下，这花了1.5秒。我与空军搜索与救援队进行了无线电联系，然后当我漂浮在地上时，看着我的飞机在农田中坠毁。我从那里降了一英里。塞尔维亚人立即向该地区泛滥。有一次，它们在我躲藏在分隔两个农田的灌溉沟的几百码内。我的装备被救生设备藏在深绿色的救生筏下。八个小时后，一架美国直升机来接我。稍后，我会发现我离被捕只有几分钟的路程。
  从头到脚，从跌倒到在田野里漫长的等待，这一切都让我想到了将我击落的塞尔维亚地对空导弹操作员。我想象着如此生动地站在他旁边，享受着他的陪伴，对他说：“真的很好。”
 十二年后，我有机会亲自告诉他。我于2006年从现役退休，并在空军与新罕布什尔州的平民一起工作。在那里，我收到了来自塞尔维亚纪录片制片人Zeljko Mirkovic的电子邮件，询问我是否要返回塞尔维亚见见被我击me的人Zoltan Dani。他想拍一部关于团圆的电影。
 我渴望见到佐尔坦。和他见面的想法让我很着迷，而不是成为对手，而是成为朋友。我需要探索和解的可能性。所以我对Zeljko说了。但是，我有一个大问题：第一次去塞尔维亚时，我正在投炸弹。我现在将如何收到？
  战争结束后，佐坦从塞尔维亚军队退役，并学会了烘烤薄片状的用于糕点的酥脆面团。制作叶序很难。 Zoltan工作时，这是一门艺术。他拉伸面团，然后将其扔到空中，巧妙地缠住它，然后一举将其张开在桌子上。然后，他再次将其拉伸直到纸薄为止。 
在他的面包店，他给我围裙和帽子，让我上班。我非常善于揉捏和拉伸面团，但是我的失败之处在于将面团扔向空中：每次尝试时，它都会裂开。那天我经历了很多生面团。
 但是佐尔坦不在乎。他让我感到舒服。有一次，我注意到他的脸上有面粉。我没有再三思，我伸手将其抹去。
  当我的课程结束并且要清理时，我告诉Zoltan我想看一下我隐藏的地方。泽尔科（Zeljko）的摄制组跟着，我们开车去了。令人惊讶的是，我在那八个辛苦的小时里找到了灌溉渠。我什至遇到了在田间工作的农民。我对被当作敌方战斗员的任何恐惧都很快得到缓解。原来我是当地的英雄。我的隐形战斗机的倒塌是该地区发生的最大事件。
 回到主人佐尔坦坚持要接管儿子的房间的时候，我向达尼一家赠送了礼物。我为孩子们带来了棒球和棒球手套，为佐尔坦带来了F-117的模型。他炸毁了一个真实的模型，我认为他需要一个模型。我的妻子劳伦（Lauren）为Zoltan的妻子艾伦（Iren）铺了被子，以示和平。最后的礼物是我四个孩子中的一个，凯根，然后是九个，他正在学习小提琴。我曾录制过他演奏塞尔维亚乐曲“ Svilen Konac”或“ Silk Thread”的记录。它过去挺美。
 我和佐尔坦开始互相了解。我发现他是一个温柔而温柔的灵魂，是一个有信仰的人，像我一样，把家人关在附近并且亲爱的。当然，我们讨论了“那一天”。
  佐尔坦当时43岁，而他击落我的那晚是40岁。他说，只要机组人员发射跟踪雷达的时间超过20秒，他们就会关闭并移动，因为那样长的时间足以让敌人（我们）知道他们的位置。如果他们做了两次，他们将不会再尝试；这太危险了。但是那天晚上，佐尔坦有一种感觉。他进行了第三次尝试，并获得了回报。他们实现了前所未有的成就-他们击落了隐形战斗机。
 几天后，我们分开了，誓言保持联系。确实，第二年，2012年，佐尔坦和他的家人来到新罕布什尔州度过了一个星期。泽尔科（Zeljko）也来了，并拍摄了这次访问。但是我们几乎没有注意到摄像机。我们是彼此相处的朋友。艾伦（Iren）向我们提供了钩编的蕾丝桌布，这是传家宝，已经存在了50年。佐尔坦给了我一个SA3导弹的手工模型。 
我于2012年回到塞尔维亚，参加了泽尔科的电影《第二次会议》的首映礼。 放映后的提问中，一位女士对我说：“当你被击落时，我庆祝。 我和朋友们一起欢呼。 但是我们为您没有被杀感到难过。 我们以为你应该死。” 您可以想象观众的安静。 然后她说：“但是现在我们已经认识了你，我很高兴你在这里。” 我在哭。  这个世界上有太多误解，导致不必要的悲伤。 在我的生活中拥有丹尼斯人（一个积极，快乐的家庭）改变了我的看法。 听起来也许有些陈词滥调，但是如果世界上所有的宗教，文化和种族群体只有一种方式，可以有意义的方式相遇并相互了解，就像佐尔坦和我所拥有的那样，那么我们怎么可能去 再次战争？
note_en:
From the moment Serbian missile commander Zoltan Dani shot down U.S. fighter pilot Dale Zelko's plane, the two were tangled in a complicated relationship of life and death.
  As soon as you leave Belgrade, you’re transported back in time. The Serbian capital is a modern metropolis, but in the countryside, donkeys still clop along cobblestones, past farmers taking their produce to market.
 I was here visiting a baker, Zoltan Dani, in 2011. The bakery, in the town of Skorenovac, is in a timeworn building next to the house where Zoltan grew up. When I walked in, I found him, covered in flour and wearing a baker’s hat and apron, stretching dough on a large table. He smiled and hustled over to greet me. I saluted him, stopping him in his tracks. He returned my salute, then we hugged. It could not have felt more natural. He was like my brother, this man who had tried to kill me 12 years earlier.
 Back then I was an F-117 Stealth Fighter pilot during the first week of the Serbian conflict. The year was 1999. My mission: Bomb the most heavily defended, high-value targets deep in enemy territory. It was a terrifying job. I knew that the people on the other side of the war felt the same. But I couldn’t afford to think about them. I tried to think of my targets as just concrete and steel, with no personal attachment, no human element. That was my survival mechanism.
 And it worked. The first night of the war, I had two targets and hit both. I flew on the third night, which was also successful. My target on the fourth night was number one on the strategic target list. The entire route was defended with heat-seeking missiles, radar-guided missiles, antiaircraft guns—a full array of nasty stuff.
 Stealth technology is not invisible technology. It just makes it harder for an aircraft to be detected. So on that fourth night, before entering Serbian airspace, I did a stealth check. I turned off lights, brought in antennae, and turned off the radio and transponder—any kind of emitter or transmitter that might give away my position. On that fourth night, I was coming up to the border, just waiting until the last moment to turn the radio off, desperate for that call: We figured it out peacefully. You can return to base. I didn’t get that radio call.
  I flew into Serbia, hit my target, and began my return back to the base in Italy. I didn’t see the two SA3 missiles until they punched through the cloud cover.
 The missiles were moving at three times the speed of sound, so there wasn’t much time to react. Just before the first missile reached me, I closed my eyes and turned my head, anticipating the impact. I knew there would be a fireball, and I didn’t want to be blinded. I felt the first one go right over me, so close that it rocked the aircraft. Then I opened my eyes and turned my head, and there was the other missile. The impact was violent. A huge flash of light and heat engulfed my plane and blew off the left wing, sending the plane into a roll.
 If you’re in an airplane that hits some turbulence and you feel a little light in your feet, you’re momentarily in zero g’s. I was at negative seven g’s. My body was being pulled out of the seat upward toward the canopy. As I strained to reach the ejection handles, one thought crossed my mind: This is really, really, really bad.
  From the moment I pulled the ejection handles to being under a fully inflated parachute took 1.5 seconds. I made radio contact with Air Force search-and-rescue teams, then, as I floated down to earth, watched my plane crash in a farm field. I landed a mile from there. The Serbs immediately flooded the area looking for me. At one point, they were within a couple of hundred yards of where I was hiding in an irrigation ditch separating two farm fields. My gear was under the dark-green life raft from my survival equipment. Eight hours later, an American helicopter came and got me. I would later learn that I had been minutes away from being captured.
  Through it all, from my fall to the long hours waiting in the field, I thought about the Serbian surface-to-air missile operator who’d shot me down. I imagined so vividly standing next to him, enjoying his company, and saying to him, “Really nice shot.”
 Twelve years later, I got the opportunity to tell him in person. I’d retired from active duty in 2006 and worked for the Air Force as a civilian in New Hampshire, where I’d moved with my family. It was there that I got an e-mail from a Serbian documentary filmmaker, Zeljko Mirkovic, asking if I’d like to return to Serbia and meet Zoltan Dani, the man who’d shot me down. He wanted to make a film about the reunion.
 I was eager to meet Zoltan. I’d become consumed by the idea of meeting him, not as an adversary but as a friend. I needed to explore the possibilities of reconciliation. So I said yes to Zeljko. I had, however, one big concern: The first time I was in Serbia, I was dropping bombs. How would I be received now?
  After the war, Zoltan retired from the Serbian army and learned to bake the thin sheets of phyllo dough used for flaky pastries. Making phyllo is hard. When Zoltan works, it’s an art. He stretches the dough, then casts it into the air, deftly snaring it, and splaying it out on the table in one motion. He then stretches it again until it’s paper-thin.
 At his bakery, he gave me an apron and a hat and put me to work. I was pretty good at kneading and stretching the dough, but my downfall came in tossing the dough into the air: Each time I tried, it ripped. I went through a lot of dough that day.
 But Zoltan didn’t care. He made me feel comfortable. At one point, I noticed he had flour on his face. Without giving it a second thought, I reached over and wiped it off.
  When my lesson was over and I’d cleaned up, I told Zoltan I wanted to see the field where I’d hidden. Followed by Zeljko’s film crew, we drove to it. Amazingly, I found the irrigation ditch where I’d spent those eight grueling hours. I even met the farmers who were working the field. Any fears I had about being treated like an enemy combatant were quickly eased. Turns out I was a local hero. The downing of my Stealth Fighter had been the biggest thing to happen in that area.
 Back at Zoltan’s home, where my host insisted I take over his son’s room, I presented gifts to the Dani family. I’d brought baseballs and baseball gloves for the kids and a model of an F-117 for Zoltan. He had blown up a real one—I figured he needed a model of it. My wife, Lauren, had made a quilt for Zoltan’s wife, Iren, as a symbol of peace. The last gift was from one of my four children, Kegan, then nine, who was learning the violin. I had recorded him playing a Serbian tune called “Svilen Konac,” or “Silk Thread.” It was beautiful.
 Zoltan and I began to get to know each other. I discovered he was a gentle, tenderhearted soul, a man of faith who, like me, held his family near and dear. And, of course, we discussed “that” day.
  Zoltan was 43 and I was 40 on the night he shot me down. He said that anytime his crew emitted their tracking radar longer than 20 seconds, they would shut down and move because that would be long enough for the enemy—us—to figure out their location. And if they did it twice, they wouldn’t try again; it was too dangerous. But that night, Zoltan had a feeling. He went for a third try, and it paid off. They accomplished what no one had ever done before—they shot down a Stealth Fighter.
 After a few days, we parted ways, vowing to keep in touch. And, indeed, the next year, 2012, Zoltan and his family came to New Hampshire for a week. Zeljko came, as well, and filmed the visit. But we barely noticed the cameras. We were friends spending time with each other. Iren presented us with a crocheted lace tablecloth, an heirloom that had been in their family for 50 years. And Zoltan gave me a handcrafted model of an SA3 missile.
   I returned to Serbia in 2012 for the premiere of Zeljko’s movie,  The Second Meeting. During questions after the screening, one woman said to me, “When you were shot down, I celebrated. I cheered with my friends. But we were upset that you were not killed. We thought you deserved to die.” You can imagine the hush in the audience. And then she said, “But now that we have gotten to know you, I’m so glad that you are here.” I was weeping.
  There’s so much misunderstanding in the world resulting in unnecessary sorrow. Having the Danis—a positive, joyful family—in my life has altered my perspective. It may sound trite, but if only there were a way for all the religious, cultural, and ethnic groups of the world to meet and get to know one another in a meaningful way—the way Zoltan and I have—how could we ever go to war again?
title: 15年后（2013年），F-117飞行员和击落他的军官相遇
title_en: An F-117 pilot and the officer who shot him down meet, 15 years later  (2013)

note:
在因努文化中，金刚狼是骗子，是许多戏剧性刺山柑的中心人物。有时候，金刚狼是英雄，它使印努族的家庭摆脱了恶毒的食人族；其他时候，他是一个流氓，欺骗Innu和他的同伴。无论哪种方式，都可以确保他陷入恶作剧。
 来自拉布拉多北部纳图西什（Natuashish）的因努（Innu）社区的电影人克里斯汀·波特（Christine Poker）在帐篷炉子旁长大，听着关于纵容库埃库特苏（Kuekuatsheu）的故事。
  对于Innu来说，Kuekuatsheu不仅仅是故事中的角色；他几乎是社区的一部分。长老仍在谈论他，好像他们自己认识他一样，仿佛亲眼目睹了他的滑稽动作。扑克说，她记得她的祖父母曾在讲故事，讲述了库卡斯图将如何从营地偷食物的故事。
 “我的祖父母说，当Innu人开始死亡并离开该国（定居在更大的社区中）时，Kuekuatsheu随他们离开了，” Poker解释说。她指出，当她与孙子孙女在一起时，他们对听金刚狼故事的兴趣降低了。但是，当它们在陆地上时，一切都会改变。年轻人在夜晚的帐篷里渴望听到关于库伊卡修的故事。
 确定金刚狼是骗子的想法从何而来并不困难。这些动物虽然隐居，但也以好奇和偷偷摸摸而闻名。众所周知，它们会从陷阱，洗劫小屋和逃避猎人的手中诱饵。如果您在野外看到金刚狼，那就算幸运了-他们倾向于避开有人居住的地区，人口密度极低并且大多数人入睡时从黄昏到黎明最活跃。
 金刚狼虽然看起来像小蝴蝶结的熊，尾巴浓密，但实际上是穆斯泰科（Mustelidae）家族的最大成员，其中包括黄鼬、,和水獭。成年雄性可重达14公斤，成年雌性可重达9公斤。它们的耳朵小，鼻子粗短，牙齿锋利，还有半可伸缩的利爪。他们也被认为凶猛，树皮和它们的叮咬一样糟糕。他们的咆哮最好被描述为狮子的吼叫声与一架小型飞机的引擎之间的交叉声。当您独自一人在树林里散步时，这是一种令人震惊的声音，这无疑增加了这种动物的神秘感。
  金刚狼在其生态系统中扮演着多面手的角色，几乎会吃掉任何东西，从浆果，植物到肉和腐肉。他们猎捕较小的动物-他们最喜欢的食物是海狸-但常常是拾荒者，吃掉了被狼吞下的较大动物的遗骸，例如驼鹿和北美驯鹿。但是，也有经证实的事实，金刚狼会长时间缠扰它们，杀死大型的有蹄类动物，例如鹿和驯鹿，耗尽它们的猎物，然后再进行杀戮。 
金刚狼最大的生存适应力之一就是咬人。头部的肌肉和骨骼结构为这种小动物提供了强大的下巴。口腔后部的特殊上臼齿向口腔内部旋转了90度，使他们可以通过骨头cho骨进入骨髓内部。他们毫不费力地撕破冻肉。因此，金刚狼的粪便很容易辨认，因为它充满了骨头碎片。
 金刚狼一年四季都很活跃，尽管像大多数森林生物一样，冬天它们比较安静。它们在初夏繁殖，并在冬末或初春生出一两个套件。他们厚厚的大衣可以保护他们免受霜冻和寒冷的侵害，它们像雪鞋一样宽的脚非常适合在深雪中穿行。他们需要深厚的积雪才能使巢穴与严酷的冬季条件隔离。这些适应措施对于金刚狼的生存至关重要，但也使它们容易受到全球变暖的影响。
 那块厚厚，温暖的皮草会排走霜，这曾经使金刚狼皮成为北美皮草交易中不可或缺的一部分。他们的皮毛通常被用来在派克大衣的引擎盖上做衬里，曾经导致动物被大量困住。他们在加拿大南部的人口从未完全康复。
  的确，尽管它们的范围曾经跨越加拿大南部的大部分地区，但现在主要分布在北部大部分地区的北方森林，山脉和冻原中。一头金刚狼平均每3到10只动物大约有1000平方公里，因此它可以维持数百平方公里的领土，每天走很远的距离巡逻以觅食并驱赶可能的竞争。雄性占优势的金刚狼将仅与它繁殖的一两只雌性重叠。
 像麝香科动物中的所有动物一样，金刚狼拥有非常发达的气味腺，它们被用来标记自己的领土以警告其他金刚狼远离。因此，与他们的家人在一起长达一年之久的金刚狼经常会走数百公里，以建立一个远离其他金刚狼的领土。
  曾经有人认为，金刚狼过着孤独的生活，只有在夏天才能繁殖。尽管这主要是事实，但使用GPS项圈追踪动物的研究人员发现，金刚狼比最初想象的更具社交性。
 加拿大野生动物保护协会的生态学家马修·斯卡福德（Matthew Scrafford）在安大略省北部研究金刚狼已有三年多了。那时，他的GPS数据显示，金刚狼父母一次都在猎杀和清理后代几天。 “他们是家庭单位，”斯卡福德解释道。 “他们正在互相学习，并在教导他们的后代。在大量优质GPS数据和相机开始出现之前，我们认为它们是孤独者-确实如此-但他们确实花了大量时间在彼此之间漫游。” 
斯卡福德说，健康的金刚狼种群是生态系统健康的有力指标，因为该物种需要从相互联系的大片完整地区收集的各种食物，换句话说，它们需要多种食物和空间漫游。
 斯卡福德说：“我们发现，就它们能够承受人类的某些发展而言，它们并不是纯粹的荒野物种。” “您确实在发达的景观和林业工作景观中发现了金刚狼。尽管它们密度低，繁殖率低，但它们并不需要纯净的荒野来生存，因此您必须注意其领土发生了什么，否则它们将不复存在。”
  如今，金刚狼面临的最大威胁是栖息地的丧失和人类的发展。研究人员正在与北部的原住民社区合作，以追踪和监视金刚狼的种群，以更好地了解人类栖息地如何影响其栖息地。骗子英雄-凶猛，聪明和坚强-将需要其所有的才智来继续蓬勃发展。
note_en:
In Innu culture, the wolverine is a trickster, the central character in many dramatic capers. Sometimes the wolverine is a hero, saving Innu families from vicious cannibals; other times he’s a scoundrel tricking both the Innu and his fellow animals. Either way, it’s guaranteed that he’s getting into mischief.
 Filmmaker Christine Poker, who comes from the Innu community of Natuashish in northern Labrador, grew up hearing stories around the tent stove about the conniving Kuekuatsheu, the name for the wolverine in her language of Innu-aimun.
  For the Innu, Kuekuatsheu is more than just a character in a story; he is almost a part of the community. Elders still talk of him as if they know him personally, as if they witnessed his antics themselves. Poker says she remembers her grandparents telling her stories of how Kuekuatsheu would steal food from their camp.
 “My grandparents said that when the Innu people started dying and leaving the country [to settle in larger communities], Kuekuatsheu left with them,” Poker explains. She notes that when she is in her community with her grandkids, they’re less interested in hearing the stories of the wolverine. But that all changes when they are on the Land. In their tent at night, the young people are eager to hear the stories of Kuekuatsheu.
 It is not difficult to determine where the idea of the wolverine being a trickster comes from. Though reclusive, these animals are also renowned for being curious and sneaky. They have been known to steal bait from traps, ransack cabins and elude hunters. If you see a wolverine in the wild, consider yourself lucky — they tend to avoid areas inhabited by people, have an extremely low population density and are most active from dusk to dawn when most people are asleep.
 Although they resemble small bow-legged bears with bushy tails, wolverines are actually the largest members of the Mustelidae family, which includes weasels, badgers and otters. Adult males can weigh up to 14 kilograms, and adult females can weigh up to nine kilograms. They have small ears, a stubby nose and sharp teeth, as well as sharp semi- retractable claws. They are also known to be ferocious, with a bark as bad as their bite. Their growl can best be described as a cross between a lion’s roar and the engine of a small plane — it’s a startling sound to hear when you’re walking alone in the woods and no doubt adds to the creature’s mystique.
  Wolverines play a generalist’s role in their ecosystem and will eat just about anything, from berries and vegetation to meat and carrion. They hunt smaller animals — their favourite meal is the beaver — but are often scavengers, eating the remains of larger animals such as moose and caribou that have been taken down by wolves. There are, however, also verified accounts of wolverines killing large ungulates such as deer and caribou by stalking them over long periods, exhausting their prey before going in for the kill.
 One of the wolverine’s greatest survival adaptations is its bite; the muscle and bone structures in their heads give them a powerful jaw for such a small creature. A special upper molar at the back of their mouths is rotated 90 degrees toward the inside of their mouth, allowing them to chomp through bone to get at the marrow inside. They have no problem tearing through frozen flesh. As a result, the scat of wolverines is easy to identify because it’s full of bone fragments.
 Wolverines are active year-round, although like most forest life they are quieter in the winter. They breed in early summer and give birth to one or two kits in the late winter or early spring. Their thick coats protect them from the frost and cold, and their wide snowshoe-like feet are perfectly adapted for moving through deep snow. They need that deep snow cover to insulate their dens from harsh winter conditions. While these adaptations are important for the wolverine’s survival, they also make them susceptible to the impacts of global warming.
 That thick, warm fur that wicks away frost once made wolverine pelts a prized part of the North American fur trade. Their fur, which was often used to line the hoods of parkas, once led to the animal being trapped in great numbers. Their populations in southern Canada have never fully recovered.
  Indeed, though their range once stretched across much of southern Canada, they are now largely found in the boreal forests, mountain ranges and tundras that stretch across much of the North. With an average of just three to 10 animals typically found in a thousand square kilometres, a single wolverine can maintain a territory of hundreds of square kilometres, walking great distances every day to patrol for food and to drive away any would-be competition. A dominant male wolverine will overlap its territory only with one or two females with which it breeds.
 Like all the animals in the Mustelidae family, wolverines have very well-developed scent glands, which they use to mark their territories to warn other wolverines to stay away. That’s why young wolverines, which remain with their family for up to a year, will often travel hundreds of kilometres to establish a territory far away from other wolverines.
  It was once believed that wolverines lived solitary lives, getting together only to breed during the summer. Although this is mainly true, researchers using GPS collars to track the animals have discovered that wolverines are more social than originally thought.
 Matthew Scrafford, an ecologist with Wildlife Conservation Society Canada, has been studying wolverines in northern Ontario for more than three years. In that time, his GPS data has shown both wolverine parents hunting and scavenging with their offspring for days at a time. “They’re a family unit,” Scrafford explains. “They’re learning from each other and teaching their off- spring. Before a lot of this good GPS data and cameras started coming in, we thought of them as loners — and they are — but they do spend a lot of time roaming around the landscape with each other.”
 A healthy wolverine population is a strong indicator of a healthy eco-system, Scrafford says, since the species requires a variety of foods gathered from large intact areas of interconnected wilderness — in other words, they need lots of types of food and lots of space to roam.
 “We have found that in as far as they can withstand some human development, they’re not purely a wilderness species,” says Scrafford. “You do find wolverines in developed landscapes and forestry working landscapes. They don’t need pure wilderness to exist, although they have low density and low reproductive rates, so you have to watch what happens to their territory, or else they’re going to blink out of existence.”
  Today, the biggest threat to wolverines is habitat loss and human development. Researchers are working with Indigenous communities across the North to track and monitor wolverine populations to better understand how their habitats are being affected by human encroachment. The trickster hero — ferocious, clever and strong — will need all of its ingenuity to continue to flourish.
title: 金刚狼：跟踪难以捉摸的骗子

note:
Ember和Agora Energiewende的第五份年度报告（追踪欧洲的电力转型）于2021年1月25日发布。该报告显示，可再生能源已超过化石燃料，成为2020年欧盟的主要电力来源。
  该报告汇总并分析了每个欧盟国家2020年全年的发电量，追踪了欧洲的电力过渡情况。这是Ember（前身为Sandbag）连续第五年与Agora Energiewende合作进行这项分析。
 2020年，可再生能源发电量占欧洲发电量的38％（2019年为34.6％），首次超过化石燃料发电量，后者下降至37％。这是欧洲清洁能源转型的重要里程碑。在国家一级，德国和西班牙（以及分别是英国）也首次实现了这一里程碑。但是，从煤炭到清洁的过渡仍然太慢，无法在2030年之前将温室气体排放量减少55％，并在2050年之前实现气候中和。
 虽然Covid-19在所有国家都有影响，但它对从化石燃料到可再生能源的总体趋势的影响非常有限。尽管发生了这种大流行，可再生能源的增长仍然令人放心，并且如果没有如此强劲的电力需求反弹和有史以来最糟糕的核能发电年，化石燃料发电的下降本来可能更加严重。
 风能和太阳能正在推动欧洲可再生能源的兴起。 2020年风力发电量增长9％，太阳能发电量增长15％。到2020年，它们共同创造了欧洲五分之一的电力。自2015年以来，风能和太阳能为欧洲可再生能源的增长提供了动力，因为生物能源增长停滞不前，水力发电保持不变。
 可再生能源的增长仍然太慢–风能和太阳能发电的增长必须接近三倍才能达到欧洲的2030年绿色交易目标：从2010-2020年的年均38 TWh到2020-2030年的年均100 TWh。令人鼓舞的是，尽管受到Covid-19的影响，风能和太阳能在2020年仍增加了51太瓦小时，远高于2010-2020年的平均水平。 IEA预测2021年风能和太阳能产能将创纪录增长。不过，欧盟国家仍需要大幅提高2030年的承诺。目前，国家能源和气候计划每年仅增加约72 TWh的新风能和太阳能，而不是每年所需的100 TWh。
 2020年煤炭产量下降了20％，自2015年以来减少了一半。几乎每个国家的煤炭产量都有所下降，持续的煤炭崩塌早在Covid-19之前就已经到位。 2020年降幅的一半归因于电力需求的下降，由于Covid-19的影响，电力需求下降了4％；一半来自额外的风能和太阳能。随着2021年电力需求反弹，如果要维持近期的煤炭下降，风能和太阳能将需要以更快的速度增长。 
尽管发生了大流行，但到2020年天然气发电量仅下降了4％。到2020年，化石下降的大部分是煤炭，而不是天然气，因为强劲的碳价意味着天然气是化石发电中最便宜的一种，甚至连几个月以来的褐煤产量都首次减少。核能发电量在2020年下降了10％，可能是有史以来最大的下降，这也阻止了天然气（以及程度较轻的煤炭）发电量进一步下降。
 这意味着2020年欧洲的电力清洁度将比2015年减少29％。碳强度已从2015年的每千瓦时317克二氧化碳减少到2020年的226克。尽管那时的煤炭产量几乎减少了一半，但煤炭产量下降抵消了43％的煤炭减少，减缓了碳的减少强度。
  风能和太阳能推动了欧洲可再生能源的兴起。到2020年，风能和太阳能发电量占欧洲电力总量的五分之一。风能发电量占欧洲电力总量的14％，高于2015年的9％；太阳能发电量所占的比例则从2015年的3％增长至5％。过去十年中，生物能源*的增长放缓自2018年以来停滞不前。新的水力发电量几乎为零，水力发电的变化仅受降水变化的驱动。
  2020年欧盟27国的风能和太阳能发电量增长了10％。荷兰风能和太阳能发电增幅最大，这是由于它在多年的缓慢部署后赶上了风口，使风能和太阳能与欧洲平均水平保持一致（发电量的19％，而欧盟27国为20％）。在法国，仅风能和太阳能就超过了化石，这标志着下一个绿色的里程碑，丹麦和瑞典已经实现了这一里程碑。增长最大的国家是荷兰（40％），瑞典（36％）和比利时（28％）。许多未在图中显示的较小国家在风能和太阳能方面仍保持近乎零的增长：奥地利，葡萄牙，捷克共和国，意大利，罗马尼亚和斯洛伐克。
  风能和太阳能领域的领导人表明，如果存在持续的政治意愿，那将有可能实现，而尽管太阳能和风能条件优越，但一些国家仍然落后。到2020年，丹麦将62％的电力来自风能和太阳能，几乎是下一个爱尔兰国家的两倍。德国位居第三，然后西班牙超过葡萄牙位居第四。自2015年以来，七个国家（其中一些太阳能和风能条件极佳）几乎没有增长-葡萄牙，罗马尼亚，奥地利，意大利，捷克，斯洛伐克和保加利亚。
   风能和太阳能发电的增长必须接近三倍，才能达到欧洲2030年达成的绿色交易目标。到2020年，风能和太阳能增加了51太瓦小时，尽管这不是创纪录的水平，但比过去十年的平均38 TWh /年高出许多。但是，要实现欧洲到2030年将温室气体排放减少55％的目标，每年的增加量将需要增加近三倍，达到每年约100 TWh。这意味着随着十年来的增长回升，到2030年本身将增加超过100 TWh。根据Ember的Vision或Division，目前国家能源和气候计划的总和仅为每年约72 TWh。报告，因此各国仍然需要加强其2030年国家承诺。
   尽管有Covid-19，风能和太阳能装置在2020年仍然出奇地强劲，而2021年可能会创下新的风能和太阳能装置记录。 IEA估计，到2020年，欧盟的风电装机容量将比2019年减少18％，但由于法国，波兰和丹麦的新增长，到2021年，风电装机容量将急剧增加。欧洲的太阳能形势仍然更加光明：国际能源署（IEA）估计，到2020年，欧盟的太阳能安装量将比2019年增加16％，这主要是由于德国，法国和波兰拍卖的公用事业规模部署，然后从2021年起急剧增加许多国家开始加强部署。 
到2020年，欧洲的电力需求下降了4％；尽管在锁定高峰期4月份下降了13％，但到冬季开始时又恢复到Covid之前的水平。 4月份降幅最大的是意大利（-21％），法国（-19％）和西班牙（-17％）。但是，这三个国家/地区的12月需求均同比增长，表明从2021年起将恢复正常需求水平。
 在这种需求急剧变化的背景下，目前尚不清楚随着2020年电气化的发展，电动汽车，热泵和电解池的增加，潜在的电力需求是否会增加。显然，这将是未来的驱动力，进一步强调需求以便更快地部署新的风能和太阳能。
  注–就本报告而言，可再生能源的分类符合IPCC，并包括生物能源。但是，生物能源对气候的影响在很大程度上取决于原料，原料的来源以及如果不将原料燃烧为能源会发生什么。当前的欧盟生物能源可持续性标准并未充分规避高风险原料，因此，在满足有关承诺的时间范围内，不能自动假定生物能源发电会为其他可再生能源（如风能和太阳能）带来类似的气候效益。巴黎协定。有关更多信息，请参阅Ember的报告：The Burning Issue（2020年6月）和Play with Fire（2019年12月）。
  2020年煤炭产量下降了20％，现在是2015年的一半。 2020年，硬煤和褐煤的发电量降至365兆瓦时，自2015年以来下降了48％。这是一项了不起的成就，相当于每年减少约3.2亿吨的二氧化碳，约占欧洲的7％。 2020年温室气体排放量。褐煤发电量在2020年下降了18％，这是自2000年以来的最大跌幅。但是这再次比硬煤的生产下降得少，而硬煤的生产现在已经大大低于褐煤的生产。到2020年，煤炭仅占欧洲电力的13％。根据欧盟委员会的影响评估，到2030年，煤炭需要降至接近零，才能达到欧盟的55％的温室气体排放目标。
  欧洲几乎每个国家的煤炭数量减少。在风能和太阳能的良好增长的帮助下，荷兰，希腊和西班牙继续为完全淘汰煤炭提供动力。欧洲最大的煤炭生产国德国在2020年的煤炭产量下降了22％，略高于欧洲平均水平的20％。德国的跌幅甚至很大，以至于从2月到7月，其每月煤炭产量都低于波兰，这使波兰成为有史以来第一次成为欧洲第一大煤炭生产国，因为波兰的煤炭产量落后于欧洲其他地区。
  煤炭下降的只有一半是由于新的风能和太阳能。自2015年以来，尽管煤炭产量减少了一半（-340 TWh），但其中只有一半被风能和太阳能（176 TWh）所替代。天然气产量的增加以及Covid-19导致的电力需求下降也加速了煤炭的下降。 2020年本身，煤炭发电量下降了90 TWh，而风能和太阳能则上升了51 TWh。
  由于法国和比利时的问题以及瑞典和德国的永久关闭，核能发电量在2020年下降了创纪录的10％。这是自1990年以来，甚至可能是有史以来的最大跌幅。甚至比2011年福岛核电站关闭后德国的核电站还要大。法国电力公司（EDF）遭受严重停电，其核发电量下降了44TWh（11％）。瑞典下降了17TWh，部分原因是因为2019年12月关闭了Ringhals1。德国下降了11TWh，原因是2019年12月关闭了Phillipsburg。比利时下降了9TWh（20％），原因是其核舰队仍然存在问题。 
随着各国逐步淘汰核能，核电发电量将继续下降：德国为2022年，比利时为2025年，西班牙为2030年，到2035年法国将其电力混合减少一半。这再次说明了迫切需要增加风能和太阳能发电量的速度。
  尽管大流行导致电力需求下降，但到2020年天然气发电量仅下降了4％。到2020年，化石燃料的大部分降幅是在煤炭而不是天然气上，因为强劲的碳价和低廉的天然气价格意味着天然气发电是化石发电中最便宜的一种形式，甚至连几个月以来的褐煤产量都首次减少。
 到2020年，化石燃料仍占欧洲电力的20％，尽管2020年天然气下降，但仍比2015年高出14％。希腊，荷兰和波兰在2020年天然气发电量增加。
 因此，尽管风能和太阳能正在取代煤炭，但还没有国家能看到风能和太阳能开始显着取代天然气发电。实际上，希腊和荷兰的天然气发电量仍在增长。
  除波兰外，化石发电量最大的国家是荷兰，希腊，爱尔兰和意大利。尽管荷兰，希腊，爱尔兰和意大利正在逐步淘汰煤炭，但它们对天然气发电的持续依赖意味着它们距离无化石能源发电还有很长的路要走。波兰和捷克共和国是仅有的两个国家，其化石发电量是可再生能源发电量的四倍以上。奥地利的可再生能源份额最高，但即使那里仍然有大量天然气可供淘汰。
   煤炭的下降意味着欧洲的电力比2015年清洁29％。碳强度已从2015年的每千瓦时317克二氧化碳下降到2020年的226克，仅2020年就下降了10％。尽管当时的煤炭产量几乎减少了一半，但煤炭产量下降抵消了煤炭下降43％的影响，减缓了碳强度的降低。尽管德国在可再生能源方面处于领先地位，但由于煤炭比例较高，其电力仍然比欧盟平均水平还脏得多。波兰的电力现在第一次成为欧洲最脏的电力，因为爱沙尼亚减少了碳密集型油页岩的产生，从而超过了爱沙尼亚的碳强度。
  该报告中的数据由Ember整理。完整的数据集可从此excel文件中获得。请将任何数据查询发送至euan@ember-climate.org。 
2000年至2018年的数据来自EUROSTAT（nrg_bal_peh）。我们将以2019年和2020年的数据为参考，对Eurostat数据发布时的状态进行“最佳观察”。为此，我们估算每年的同比变化并将其添加到2018年的欧盟统计局数据中。要估算这些变化，我们主要依靠ENTSO-E数据提供的Ember欧洲每月月度整理​​数据，并对这些数据进行交叉检查根据欧盟统计局的任何可用月度数据和国家TSO的数据进行更改。对于德国，我们多年来一直使用AG-Energiebilanzen。注意：所有使用的数据均为“毛收入”而非“净收入”。
 与往常一样，Entso-E数据远非完美，因此估算年度变化的过程是将多个数据源拼凑在一起的一种艺术。 Entso-E的显着问题是NL和IT中化石发电的分类，以及大多数国家/地区对小型天然气和生物能源工厂的覆盖不佳。
  灰烬是一种独立的，非营利性的环境，能源智囊团。我们从欧洲气候基金会和其他慈善组织获得资助。

note_en:
Ember and Agora Energiewende’s fifth annual report tracking Europe’s electricity transition was published on 25th January 2021. It revealed that renewables overtook fossil fuels to become the EU’s main source of electricity for the first time in 2020.
  This report compiles and analyses the full-year 2020 electricity generation of every EU country, tracking Europe’s electricity transition. This is the fifth year in a row that Ember (formerly as Sandbag) has done this analysis in conjunction with Agora Energiewende.
 Renewables rose to generate 38% of Europe’s electricity in 2020 (compared to 34.6% in 2019), for the first time overtaking fossil-fired generation, which fell to 37%. This is an important milestone in Europe’s Clean Energy Transition. At a country level, Germany and Spain ( and separately the UK) also achieved this milestone for the first time. The transition from coal to clean is, however, still too slow for reaching 55% greenhouse gas reductions by 2030 and climate neutrality by 2050.
 While Covid-19 had an impact in all countries, its impact on the overall trend from fossil fuels to renewables was quite limited. The rise in renewables was reassuringly robust despite the pandemic, and the fall in fossil-fired electricity could have been even more dramatic, had it not been for such a bounce-back in electricity demand and the worst year on record for nuclear generation.
 Wind and solar are powering Europe’s renewables rise . Wind generation rose 9% in 2020 and solar generation rose 15%. Together they generated a fifth of Europe’s electricity in 2020. Since 2015, wind and solar have supplied all of Europe’s growth in renewables, as bioenergy growth has stalled, and hydro generation remains unchanged.
 Renewables rise is still too slow – wind and solar generation growth must nearly triple to reach Europe’s 2030 green deal targets:  from 38 TWh per year average growth in 2010-2020 to 100 TWh per year average growth between 2020-2030. It is encouraging that wind and solar increased by 51 terawatt-hours in 2020, well above the 2010-2020 average, despite facing some impact from Covid-19. The IEA forecast record wind and solar capacity growth in 2021. Still, EU countries need to step up their 2030 commitments considerably. At the moment, national energy and climate plans only add up to about 72 TWh new wind and solar per year, not the 100 TWh/year that are needed.
 Coal generation fell 20% in 2020, and has halved since 2015 . Coal generation fell in almost every country, continuing coal’s collapse that was well in place before Covid-19. Half of the drop in 2020 was due to a decrease in electricity demand, which fell by 4% due to the impact of Covid-19; and half was from additional wind and solar. As electricity demand bounces back in 2021, wind and solar will need to rise at a faster rate if the recent falls in coal are to be sustained.
 Gas generation fell only 4% in 2020, despite the pandemic . Most of the fall in fossil was on coal rather than gas in 2020, because a robust carbon price meant gas generation was the cheapest form of fossil generation, even undercutting lignite for the first time in some months. Nuclear generation fell by 10% in 2020 – probably the largest fall ever – and that also kept gas (and to a lesser-extent coal) generation from falling further.
 This means Europe’s electricity in 2020 was 29% cleaner than in 2015 . Carbon intensity has fallen from 317 grams of CO2 per kilowatt-hour in 2015 to 226 grams in 2020. Although coal generation has almost halved in that time, 43% of the coal decline has been offset by increased gas generation, slowing the reduction in carbon intensity.
  Wind and solar have powered Europe’s renewables rise . Wind and solar generated a fifth of Europe’s electricity in 2020. Wind supplied 14% of Europe’s electricity, up from 9% in 2015, and solar supplied 5%, up from 3% in 2015. Bioenergy* growth slowed throughout the last decade and has stalled since 2018. New hydro capacity is near-zero, the changes in hydro generation are driven only by precipitation changes.
  Wind and solar generation grew by 10% in the EU-27 in 2020 . Netherlands saw the largest rise in wind and solar generation, as it catches up after years of slow deployment, bringing its wind and solar in line with the European average (19% of electricity production compared to 20% for the EU-27). In France wind and solar alone overtook fossils for the first time ever, marking the next green milestone, which is already met by Denmark and Sweden. The biggest increases were in the Netherlands (40%), Sweden (36%) and Belgium (28%). Many of the smaller countries not on the graphic continued to see near-zero growth in wind and solar: Austria, Portugal, Czech Republic, Italy, Romania and Slovakia.
  Leaders in wind and solar show what is possible if there is sustained political will, while some countries continue to lag behind despite excellent solar and wind conditions.  Denmark generated 62% of its electricity from wind and solar in 2020, which was almost twice the next country of Ireland. Germany was third, and then Spain overtook Portugal into fourth place. Seven countries – some with excellent conditions for solar and wind – have barely seen any growth since 2015 – Portugal, Romania, Austria, Italy, Czechia, Slovakia and Bulgaria.
   Wind and solar generation growth must nearly triple to reach Europe’s 2030 green deal targets . Wind and solar increased by 51 terawatt-hours in 2020, and whilst it wasn’t a record, it was well-above the 38 TWh/year average last decade. But annual additions will need to nearly triple to around 100 TWh/year to reach Europe’s 2030 target of 55% cuts to GHG emissions. That means well over 100 TWh added in 2030 itself, as growth picks up through the decade. Currently national energy and climate plans add up to only about 72 TWh/year, according to Ember’s   Vision or Division? report , so countries still need to step up their 2030 national commitments.
   Wind and solar installations were surprisingly robust in 2020 despite Covid-19, and 2021 might see record new wind and solar installations . The IEA estimates there will be 18% less   wind  capacity installed in the European Union in 2020 than in 2019, but that it will sharply increase in 2021 due to new growth in France, Poland and Denmark. Europe’s solar picture is brighter still: the IEA estimates that   solar  installed in the European Union will increase by 16% in 2020 compared to 2019 due mostly to utility-scale deployment from auctions in Germany, France and Poland, and then dramatically increase from 2021 as many countries begin to step up deployment.
  Europe’s electricity demand was down 4% in 2020; although it was down 13% in April at the height of the lockdown, it then recovered to pre-Covid levels by the start of winter.  The deepest reductions in April were in Italy (-21%), France (-19%) then Spain (-17%). However, all three countries recorded higher year-on-year demand in December, indicating a bounceback to normal demand levels from 2021.
 Against this backdrop of dramatic demand shifts, it’s not clear whether underlying electricity demand increased as electrification steps up with more electric cars, heat pumps and electrolysers in 2020. What is clear is that this will be a driver in the future, further underscoring the need for more rapid deployment of new wind and solar.
  Note – for the purposes of this report, renewables are classified in line with the IPCC and include bioenergy. However, the climate impact of bioenergy is highly dependant on the feedstock, how it was sourced and what would have happened had the feedstock not been burnt for energy. The current EU bioenergy sustainability criteria do not sufficiently regulate out high-risk feedstocks and therefore electricity generation from bioenergy cannot be automatically assumed to deliver similar climate benefits to other renewables sources (such as wind and solar) over timescales relevant to meeting the commitments of the Paris Agreement. For more information please see Ember’s reports:   The Burning Issue (June 2020)  and   Playing with Fire (December 2019) .
  Coal generation fell 20% in 2020, and is now half that of 2015 . Coal generation – hard coal and lignite – fell to 365 terawatt-hours in 2020, a fall of 48% since 2015. That is a remarkable achievement and is equivalent to around 320 million tonnes less CO2 per year, which is about 7% of Europe’s GHG emissions in 2020. Lignite generation fell 18% in 2020, its biggest fall since at least 2000; but once again this was less of a fall than hard coal generation, which is now substantially below lignite generation. Coal supplied just 13% of Europe’s electricity in 2020. Coal would need to fall to near-zero by 2030 to reach the EU’s 55% GHG emissions target,   according to  the European Commission’s impact assessment.
  Europe saw coal fall in almost every country . Netherlands, Greece and Spain continue to power towards phasing out coal completely, helped by good growth in wind and solar. Germany – Europe’s largest coal generator – saw its coal generation fall by 22% in 2020, slightly faster than Europe’s average of 20%. Germany even fell so much that from February to July its monthly coal generation was below that of Poland, briefly making Poland Europe’s #1 coal generator for the first time ever, as Poland’s falls in coal lag behind the rest of Europe.
  Only half of coal’s fall was due to new wind and solar . Since 2015, although coal generation halved, (-340 TWh), only half of that was replaced by wind and solar (176 TWh). Increasing gas generation, and the fall in electricity demand due to Covid-19, also sped up coal’s fall. In 2020 itself, coal generation fell by 90 TWh, compared to a 51 TWh rise in wind and solar.
  Nuclear generation fell a record 10% in 2020, as French and Belgian problems combined with permanent closures in Sweden and Germany . This was the biggest fall since at least 1990, and possibly ever; it’s even bigger than 2011 when Germany closed nuclear plants following Fukushima. France’s nuclear generation fell by 44TWh (11%) as EDF suffered big outages. Sweden fell by 17TWh in part because of the closure of Ringhals 1 in December 2019. Germany fell by 11TWh due the closure of Phillipsburg in December 2019. Belgium fell by 9TWh (20%) as problems persisted with its nuclear fleet.
 Nuclear generation will continue to fall as countries step up to phase-out nuclear: 2022 for Germany, 2025 for Belgium, 2030 for Spain, and a reduction in France by 2035 to half of its electricity mix. This illustrates once more the immediate need to increase the speed of adding wind and solar capacities.
  Gas generation only fell 4% in 2020, despite the electricity demand drop from the pandemic . Most of the fall in fossil fuels was on coal rather than gas in 2020, because a robust carbon price and low gas prices meant gas generation was the cheapest form of fossil generation, even undercutting lignite for the first time in some months.
 Fossil gas still supplied 20% of Europe’s electricity in 2020, and although gas fell in 2020, it is still 14% higher than in 2015. Greece, the Netherlands and Poland saw gas generation rise in 2020.
 So although wind and solar is replacing coal, no country has yet seen wind and solar starting to significantly replace gas generation. And indeed Greece and the Netherlands have still seen rising gas generation.
  Besides Poland, the countries with the largest share of fossil generation were Netherlands, Greece, Ireland and Italy . Although coal is being phased out in Netherlands, Greece, Ireland and Italy, their continued reliance on gas generation means they are still a very long way from fossil-free electricity. Poland and the Czech Republic are the only two countries with more than four times the amount of fossil generation as renewables generation. Austria has the highest renewables share, but even there still has a lot of gas to phase out.
   Coal’s fall means Europe’s electricity is 29% cleaner than in 2015 . Carbon intensity has fallen from 317 grams of CO2 per kilowatt-hour in 2015 to 226 grams in 2020, falling by 10% in 2020 alone. Although coal generation has almost halved in that time, 43% of the coal decline has been offset by increased gas generation, slowing the reduction in carbon intensity. Although  Germany is leading the way on renewables, its electricity still remains much dirtier than the EU average due to a relatively high share of coal. For the first time, Poland’s electricity is now the dirtiest in Europe, overtaking Estonia’s carbon intensity as Estonia reduced its carbon-intensive oil shale generation.
  The data in this report is curated by Ember. The full dataset is available from this excel file. Please address any data queries to   euan@ember-climate.org .
 For 2000 to 2018, the data is from EUROSTAT (nrg_bal_peh). 2019 and 2020 data is our ‘best view’ of what the Eurostat data will be when it is published. We do this by estimating the year-on-year changes for each year, and adding them onto the 2018 Eurostat figures.To estimate these changes we rely mainly on Ember’s monthly curated data for Europe from ENTSO-E data, and cross-check these changes against any available monthly data from Eurostat, and data from national TSOs. For Germany we use AG-Energiebilanzen for all years. Note: all data used is “gross” not “net”.
 As always, Entso-E data is far from perfect, so the process of estimating yearly changes is an art of piecing together multiple data sources. Notable issues with Entso-E are the classification of fossil generation in NL and IT, and poor coverage of small gas and bioenergy plants across most countries.

您的日历看起来像瑞士奶酪吗？一整周的会议分散在一起，彼此之间只有1-2小时的间隔。
  这曾经是我的日历。每个黑匣子都是一次会议。如果您想知道我什么时候上班，答案是晚上（晚上10点至凌晨1点）和周末。当然，那不是可扩展的。所以我修好了。
  瑞士奶酪日历会在不同的工作优先级之间造成大量的上下文切换。在一次会议结束而另一次会议开始时，已经没有时间进行这项工作了。会议之间只有1-2小时的间隔是不够的。
 会议结束后，我们需要时间清理头脑，重新设置工作并进入工作区域，尤其是在工作需要诸如编码或设计甚至是写电子邮件之类的认知工作时。
 现在，有些人（担任领导/管理职务）可能会认为参加会议是工作。即使是这样，我还是要说他们仍然需要时间写下/跟进会议笔记，仔细思考会议期间出现的问题，赶上电子邮件或花点时间刷新一下。
 瑞士奶酪日历不允许这种情况发生。一天很快结束，一周也结束了，没有工作完成。
  第1步：确定4个小时的工作块，即工作效率最高的时间。 
对于某些人来说，是早晨，对于其他人来说，则是下午。我是一个下午的人。无论哪种方式，我们都在寻找一个4小时的时间段，以便我们不会被打扰。选择上午8点至中午12点或下午1点至下午5点。
  确保在正常工作时间（即上午8点至下午5点）挑选一个工作块。避免选择工作区，例如上午6点至10点或上午10点至下午2点（在午餐时间）或下午3点至晚上7点。记住，我们要避免下班时间。
 有时，工作块将在早晨，而其他则在晚上。没关系。尝试找到尽可能多的工作块，但要针对自己进行优化。
 尝试进行4小时的工作。 3小时可以工作，但5小时不切实际。以我的经验，4小时是最有效的。
  尝试每周工作的每一天。在最坏的情况下，每周尝试三天或10-12个小时的工作时间。十小时仅占每周40小时工作的25％。这不是一个大问题。
  根据您的经验水平，每周的天数可能会有所不同。但是，初级成员应该能够每周获得五个工作块。
  立即打开您的日历，看看它在3周内有多忙。我的猜测并不多。到了开始安排周期性4小时工作块的时间了。确保将它们标记为忙并以红色标记。使用表情符号🛑进行强调。 
某些会议可能与您的工作块冲突。 如果您是所有者，请将这些会议移至工作日开始（上午10:00）或工作日结束（下午4:00）。 如果您不是所有者，请查看是否可以请求重新安排。  当某人安排的会议预定您两次时，您很可能是第二次会议的可选参加者。 如果您是必需的与会者，他们会询问他们是否可以安排您的工作范围，或者他们会找到适合您的其他时间。  我希望这有助于保存您的日历。 请记住，您的日历就是您的时间，只有您自己才能决定如何度过。  如果您的同事的日历是瑞士式的，请考虑与他们分享此帖子。
note_en:
Does your calendar look like a swiss-cheese? A bunch of meetings spread out throughout the week with only 1-2 hours of gaps in between them.
  This use to be my calendar. Every black box was a meeting. If you wonder when I did work, the answer was in the evenings (10 pm-1 am) and on weekends. Of course, that wasn't scalable. So I fixed it.
  Swiss-cheese calendar causes a massive amount of  context-switch between different work priorities. By the time one meeting ends and another one starts, there isn't any time left to do the work. Even a 1-2 hours gap between meetings is not enough.
 When a meeting ends, we need time to clear the head, reset and get in the zone to do the work, especially when the job requires a cognitive effort like coding or design or even writing an email.
 Now some people (in leadership/management roles) may argue that attending meetings is work. Even if that's true, I would say they still need time to write down/follow-up on the meeting notes, think through the problem that surfaced during the meeting, catch up on emails, or take a mental break to refresh.
 Swiss-cheese calendar doesn't allow that to happen. Soon the day ends, and so does the week, and no work gets completed.
  Step 1: Identify the 4-hour work block—the time when you are most productive.
 For some people, it's mornings, and for others, it's afternoon. I am an afternoon person. Either way, we are looking for a 4-hour time block when we cannot be disturbed. Pick either 8 am - 12 noon or 1 pm - 5 pm.
  Make sure to pick a work block during regular work hours, i.e., 8 am - 5 pm. Avoid picking work blocks like 6 am - 10 am or 10 am - 2 pm (over lunchtime) or 3 pm - 7 pm. Remember, we want to avoid working off-hours.
 Somedays, the work block will be in the morning and others in the evening. That's ok. Try to find as many work blocks as you can but optimize for yourself.
 Try to have a 4-hour work block. 3-hours may work but 5-hours isn't practical. In my experience, 4-hours works for most.
  Try to get a work block every day of the week. In the worst case, try for three days or 10-12 hours of work block per week. Ten hours is only 25% of the 40-hour workweek. It's not a big ask.
  Depending on your experience level, the number of days per week may vary. But junior members should be able to get five work blocks per week.
  Open your calendar right now and see how busy it is in 3 weeks. My guess is not that much. That's the time to start scheduling recurring 4-hour work blocks. Make sure to mark them as busy and in the color RED. Use emoji 🛑 for emphasis.
  Some meetings may conflict with your work block. If you are the owner, move those meetings towards the beginning (10:00 am) or the end of the workday (4:00 pm). If you are not the owner, see if you can request re-scheduling.
      When someone schedules a meeting that double books you, most likely you are the optional attendee in 2nd meeting. If you were the required attendee, they would ask if they can schedule over your work block, or they will find a different time that works for you.
 I hope this helps save your calendar. Remember, your calendar is your time and only you SHOULD decide how you spend it.
 If you have a colleague whose calendar is swiss-cheesed, please consider sharing this post with them.

威胁建模正在分析系统的表示形式，以突出显示对安全性和隐私特征的关注。
        当执行威胁建模时，您开始认识到系统中可能出什么问题。它还使您可以查明需要缓解的设计和实现问题，无论是在系统的早期还是整个生命周期中。威胁模型的输出（称为威胁）将告知您可能在后续设计，开发，测试和部署后阶段中做出的决策。
    您。大家。任何关心其系统的隐私，安全性和安全性的人。
    使用宣言作为指导，以开发或完善最适合您需求的方法。我们相信，遵循宣言中的指导将导致更有效，更高效的威胁建模。反过来，这将帮助您成功开发更安全的应用程序，系统和组织，并保护它们免受对您的数据和服务的威胁。宣言包含想法，但不是方法，并且与方法无关。
  威胁建模宣言采用了与敏捷宣言类似的格式，它确定了以下两个准则：
  价值：威胁建模中的价值具有相对价值，优点或重要性。也就是说，尽管右侧的项目有价值，但我们更重视左侧的项目。
  原则：原则描述了威胁建模的基本原理。共有三种类型的原则：（i）能够成功进行威胁建模的基本，主要或一般事实，（ii）强烈建议使用的模式，以及（iii）应避免的反模式。 
威胁建模的最佳用途是通过早期和频繁的分析来提高系统的安全性和隐私性。
  威胁建模必须与组织的开发实践保持一致，并遵循迭代中的设计更改，每个迭代的范围仅限于系统的可管理部分。
    对话是建立可带来价值的共识的关键，而文档则记录了这些共识并可以进行评估。
   使用可提高生产率，增强工作流程，实现可重复性并提供可测量性的工具来支持您的方法。
 使用成功的，经过现场测试的，符合当地需求的技术，这些技术会根据对这些技术的优势和局限性的最新思考而得到借鉴。
    威胁建模不依赖于人的先天能力或独特的思维方式；每个人都可以并且应该这样做。
    不要忽视全局，因为模型的各个部分可能是相互依赖的。避免过分注意对手，资产或技术。 
最好创建多个威胁建模表示，因为没有单一的理想视图，并且其他表示可能会阐明不同的问题。
 我们制定《威胁建模宣言》的目的是，以一种可以告知，教育和启发其他从业者采用威胁建模的方式，分享我们集体威胁建模知识的精简版本，并在开发过程中提高安全性和隐私性。
  我们在思考，执行，教学和发展威胁建模实践方面积累了多年的经验，从而开发了该宣言。我们拥有行业专家，学者，作者，动手专家和演示者的不同背景。我们汇集了有关威胁建模的各种观点。我们正在进行的对话，重点关注导致威胁建模取得最佳结果的条件和方法，以及当我们失败时如何进行纠正，这些对话继续在塑造我们的想法。
  我们提供了宣言的PDF版本，并且该网站也易于打印。
note_en:
Threat modeling is analyzing representations of a system to highlight concerns about security and privacy characteristics.
        When you perform threat modeling, you begin to recognize what can go wrong in a system. It also allows you to pinpoint design and implementation issues that require mitigation, whether it is early in or throughout the lifetime of the system. The output of the threat model, which are known as threats, informs decisions that you might make in subsequent design, development, testing, and post-deployment phases.
    You. Everyone. Anyone who is concerned about the privacy, safety, and security of their system.
    Use the Manifesto as a guide to develop or refine a methodology that best fits your needs. We believe that following the guidance in the Manifesto will result in more effective and more productive threat modeling. In turn, this will help you to successfully develop more secure applications, systems, and organizations and protect them from threats to your data and services. The Manifesto contains ideas, but is not a how-to, and is methodology-agnostic.
  The Threat Modeling Manifesto follows a similar format to that of the  Agile Manifesto by identifying the two following guidelines:
  Values: A value in threat modeling is something that has relative worth, merit, or importance. That is, while there is value in the items on the right, we value the items on the left more.
  Principles: A principle describes the fundamental truths of threat modeling. There are three types of principles: (i) fundamental, primary, or general truths that enable successful threat modeling, (ii) patterns that are highly recommended, and (iii) anti-patterns that should be avoided.
  The best use of threat modeling is to improve the security and privacy of a system through early and frequent analysis.
  Threat modeling must align with an organization’s development practices and follow design changes in iterations that are each scoped to manageable portions of the system.
    Dialog is key to establishing the common understandings that lead to value, while documents record those understandings, and enable measurement.
   Support your approach with tools that allow you to increase your productivity, enhance your workflows, enable repeatability and provide measurability.
 Use successfully field-tested techniques aligned to local needs, and that are informed by the latest thinking on the benefits and limits of those techniques.
    Threat modeling does not depend on one’s innate ability or unique mindset; everyone can and should do it.
    Do not lose sight of the big picture, as parts of a model may be interdependent. Avoid exaggerating attention on adversaries, assets, or techniques.
  It is better to create multiple threat modeling representations because there is no single ideal view, and additional representations may illuminate different problems.
 Our intention for the Threat Modeling Manifesto is to share a distilled version of our collective threat modeling knowledge in a way that should inform, educate, and inspire other practitioners to adopt threat modeling as well as improve security and privacy during development.
  We developed this Manifesto after years of experience thinking about, performing, teaching, and developing the practice of, Threat Modeling. We have diverse backgrounds as industry professionals, academics, authors, hands-on experts, and presenters. We bring together varied perspectives on threat modeling. Our ongoing conversations, which focus on the conditions and approaches that lead to the best results in threat modeling, as well as how to correct when we fail, continue to shape our ideas.
  We provide a  PDF version of the manifesto and this website is also  print friendly.

note:
书籍很棒，但是我们必须购买书籍和追踪阅读的选择非常糟糕。我们中的许多人都被亚马逊生态系统所束缚-在Amazon.com上购买书籍，然后在Kindle上阅读。亚马逊悄悄收购了AbeBooks和Goodreads等网站。甚至LibraryThing现在也由亚马逊部分拥有。
 亚马逊体现了这样一种观念，即法律要求公司必须“最大化股东价值”-这是米尔顿·弗里德曼（Milton Friedman）在1970年代发明的完全虚假的说法，但仍然是许多美国公司的指导原则。该公司从书籍开始，因为它们具有商业意义，他们收购了Goodreads来读取数据，现在由于无聊或恶意而杀死了其生态系统。亚马逊从不关心书籍。
 其他公司不能仅凭商誉行事，但有很多公司对阅读有更浓厚的兴趣，并且业务往来不那么残酷。
 我拥有自己的定制系统来跟踪我的阅读，但是为书本制作Jekyll / YAML设置并不是每个人的功劳。在这个小网站上，有很多我永远无法拥有的社交功能。
   独立发行的书刊将来自许多本地书店的数据汇总在一起，以弄清楚在哪里可以买到本地书。从那里开始，您将使用书店的网站-无论是支持在线订购还是取货。
  Bookshop是最新的一家尝试为当地书店购买提供无缝有趣体验的公司，并且他们在这方面做得很好。当然，走过古朴的书店的过道可以使您接触到意想不到的新书，但是对于购买一本特定的书，亚马逊在便利性方面拥有优势。
 通过Bookshop，您可以避开Amazon和支持书店，尽管这有点间接。您选择一家本地书店，并从中获得一部分销售，但实际上不是提供书的销售。一些书店已经推迟了这种模式，说它削减了他们的当面销售。 
我认为这是一个净收益：它确实转移了亚马逊的在线销售，并且支持本地书店。如果您当地的书店有货或您想取货，但Indiebound是更好的选择，但它不能直接替代Amazon-我认为Bookshop可以。一些书店似乎喜欢它-我当地的商店Dog Eared Books，急切地链接到其Bookshop页面。
  书签有点像用于书籍的Metacritic，是值得信赖的书评者的聚集者，您可以参考这些书评者以快速了解书的接受程度。
 书签和书签是一件很酷的事情Bookshop两者都是他们热切支持嵌入其评论内容的其他网站-因此，您可以在Bookshop上查看来自“书签”的书籍的评分。如果需要，可以在我的评论中添加一个书签（或Bookshop）小部件。这种开放性和共享水平在当今的网络中已不常见。
  斜体字是一个相对较新的名词：用读书俱乐部取代Goodreads。我已经与背后的人聊天了-我认为这是一个很棒的主意。
 有很多项目试图填补Goodreads即将离开的空白。我认为，斜体类型可能是社交阅读的未来，其中社交群体只是一个读书俱乐部。互联网上的社交互动是一个臭名昭著的领域，但是保持小组规模小和背负现实关系似乎是避免上下文崩溃的一种非常聪明的方法。
 其他项目正在努力迎合更多的单身读者-例如，readng是朝着这个方向发展的一个项目。它们都还很早，很难说哪一种会成为标准。
  大多数与书籍相关的网站都严重依赖第三方数据源和现有公司。 OpenLibrary是Internet Archive的一个项目，其目标远不止于此：OpenLibrary是数据源，是现有书籍的参考。在这里，您可以实际阅读书籍，在网站上或聆听书籍。您可以下载电子书。他们具有访问作者和发布者数据的API。书套的照片。 
如果OpenLibrary成功，那么阅读的世界将完全不同：您将能够像Goodreads一样构建创意项目，而不必担心依赖某些公司或WorldCat的数据。无论您身在何处，都可以通过从OpenLibrary中检出许多书籍来阅读许多书籍。
 实现OpenLibrary的目标将非常艰巨：书籍是我见过的最复杂，稀缺和特定的数据集之一。原始数据可能很难获取，并且需要大量重复数据删除才能将不同的图书标识与同一工作相关联。对于我现在的书交叉引用，我在使用OL时使用WorldCat和OpenLibrary-不幸的是，很多时候OpenLibrary没有使用。
 但这是目前最有趣的项目之一，作为一个开源项目，任何人都可以帮助它。
    书店Indiebound正在解决购买平装书的问题。精装书。但是，我还需要其他东西：电子书的好去处。它可能是：
 公平-您预先知道作者赚了您所付的大部分钱
 这个还不存在。书籍世界似乎并不存在使Bandcamp成为可能的条件：在没有出版商的情况下可以制作高质量书籍的作者数量减少了，书籍出版商更不愿（或不太愿意）通过新渠道进行销售。
 对于某些书籍，OpenLibrary提供了这种体验。但是对于本地图书馆无法借给我的Libby应用程序的新发行版本和书籍，我想为此付费。我为自己的音乐付费-主要是在Bandcamp上，并且愿意为书本付费，因为我知道作者多年的努力得到了丰厚的回报。
note_en:
Books are amazing, but the options we have to buy books and track our reading are terrible. A lot of us are locked into the Amazon ecosystem - buying books on Amazon.com, reading them on Kindles. Sites like AbeBooks and Goodreads were quietly acquired by Amazon. Even LibraryThing is now part-owned by Amazon.
 Amazon embodies the idea that companies are legally required to “maximize shareholder value” - a  total falsehood invented in  the 1970s by Milton Friedman but nevertheless a guiding principle for a lot of American corporations. The company started with books because  they made business sense, and they  acquired Goodreads for the reading data, and are now  killing its ecosystem out of boredom or malice. Amazon has never cared about books.
 Other companies can’t operate out of goodwill alone, but there are plenty of companies with more actual interest in reading, and with a less ruthless set of business practices.
 I have my  own bespoke system for tracking my reads, but crafting a Jekyll/YAML setup for books isn’t everyone’s cup of tea. And there are many social features I’ll never get on my own in this little website.
   Indiebound stiches together data from lots of local bookstores to figure out where you can get a book locally. From there on, you’ll use the bookstore’s site - whether they support ordering online or picking it up.
  Bookshop is the latest company to try and create a seamless and fun experience for buying from local bookstores, and they’re doing a pretty great job of it. Walking through the aisles of a quaint bookstore can turn you on to unexpected new books, sure, but for getting  one particular book, Amazon has had an edge in convenience.
 Bookshop lets you avoid Amazon and support bookstores – though it’s a bit indirect. You select a local bookstore and the receive a portion of the sale, but aren’t actually the ones supplying the books.  Some bookstores have pushed back on the model, saying that it cuts into their in-person sales.
 I think it’s a net positive: it does divert online sales from Amazon, and it supports local bookstores. Indiebound is a better option if your local bookstore delivers or you’d like to pick things up, but isn’t a direct replacement for Amazon - which I think Bookshop can be. Some book stores seem to like it - my local shop  Dog Eared Books, links to their Bookshop page eagerly.
  Book Marks is sort of like  Metacritic for books - an aggregator of trusted book reviewers that you can reference to quickly get a gauge of a book’s reception.
 One thing that’s really cool with Book Marks & Bookshop both is that they eagerly support other sites embedding their review content - so you can see ratings of books on Bookshop that come from Book Marks. I could add a Book Marks (or Bookshop) widget to my reviews, if I wanted to. It’s a level of openness and sharing that’s uncommon in today’s web.
  Italic Type is a relative newcomer: replacing Goodreads with a focus on book clubs. I’ve chatted a bit with folks behind it - and I think it’s a cool idea.
 There are a quite a few projects trying to fill the void that Goodreads is leaving. Italic Type, I think, might be the future of  social reading, in which the social group is simply a book club. Social interaction on the internet is a notoriously fraught area, but keeping groups both small and piggy-backing on real-life relationships seems like a really smart way of avoiding  context collapse.
 Other projects are trying to cater to more solo readers -  readng, for example, is one project in that direction. They’re all very early on and it’s hard to say which if any will emerge as the standard.
  Most book-related websites rely heavily on third-party data sources and pre-existing companies.  OpenLibrary, a project of the  Internet Archive, is aiming for a lot more than that: OpenLibrary is a datasource, a reference for what books exist. It’s a place where you can actually read books, on the website, or listen to them. You can download eBooks. They have an API to access data about authors and publishers. Photos of book covers.
 If OpenLibrary succeeds, the world of reading will be totally different: you would be able to build creative projects in the vein of Goodreads without nervously relying on data from some corporation or from WorldCat. You’d be able to read a lot of books by checking them out of OpenLibrary, regardless of where in the world you are.
 Achieving OpenLibrary’s goal is going to be hard: books are one of the most complex, scarce, and specific datasets I’ve ever seen. The raw data can be hard to get, and there’s a tremendous amount of de-duplication required to associate different book identifiers with the same work. For my book cross-referencing now, I use  WorldCat and OpenLibrary when OL has it - and unfortunately, a lot of times OpenLibrary doesn’t.
 But this is one of the most interesting projects out there, and as an open source project, it’s one that anyone can help with.
    Bookshop & Indiebound are solving the problem of buying paperback & hardcover books. But there’s something more that I want: a good place for eBooks. It could be:
 Fair - you know up-front that authors are earning most of the money you pay
 This doesn’t exist yet. It seems like the conditions that make Bandcamp possible aren’t present in the world of books: there are fewer authors who can make quality books without a publisher, and book publishers are more hesitant (or less able) to sell through new channels.
 For some books, OpenLibrary gives this experience. But for new releases and books that my local library can’t  lend me through the Libby app, I  want to pay for them. I pay for my music - mostly on  Bandcamp, and would be happy to pay for books, knowing that the authors are being fairly rewarded for their years of effort.
title: 新的阅读堆栈
